calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-23-2026 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Mar-02-2026 Set For Case Management Conference On May-20-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File An Amended Answer; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Joel Williams In Support Thereof
Off Calendar Jan-22-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO. INC.s MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File An Amended Answer.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The motion for leave to file an amended answer is granted. Defendant shall file the proposed amended answer attached as Exhibit A to the Williams Declaration forthwith and no later than January 30, 2026.

Leave to amend a pleading is liberally granted and generally is refused only where inexcusable delay and prejudice exist. Here, there is unreasonable delay; plaintiff's complaint provides sufficient information about connection problems that defendant was on at least inquiry notice that plaintiff raised an infotainment system issue that might be covered by the release in Jimmy Banh v. American Honda Motor Co., Inc., Case No. 2:19-cv-5984. However, there is no cognizable prejudice. Plaintiff contends that there is necessary discovery about whether plaintiff is a member of the class or received notice of opt-out rights. The question of a preclusive effect of a prior judgment is generally a question of law, not a question of fact. The only fact question is whether plaintiff is a settlement class member, defined in the settlement agreement as "all current owners and lessees of Settlement Class Vehicles and former owners and lessees who file Claims but who have not otherwise already resolved and released their claims and who do not opt out of this Settlement. (Williams Decl., Ex. C.) This is not a question that requires discovery. The court also notes that, although plaintiff's papers say fact discovery is currently closed (Ardi Decl., para. 13), the order continuing the case for trial kept discovery open. (Order on Plaintiff's Ex Parte Application, Oct. 17, 2025 [All related dates concerning fact discovery are continued based on the newly set trial date])

A proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling has been submitted.

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Oct-17-2025 Continued To Mar-02-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-06-2024 Set For Trial On Nov-03-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
